Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 868, "C00": 822, "B35": 783, "A77": 65, "A03": 262, "A04": 121, "C54": 508, "C01": 780, "A87": 461, "B32": 484, "B86": 668, "B33": 389, "A10": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond with a structured message. Must include STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning for first message we send. We currently have to send a message, could be PROPOSAL or something else. We must include ARGUMENT. We refused earlier; we must include ARGUMENT somewhere. So we can reply with REFUSE and include ARGUMENT with an empty set or some text? The format requires set syntax: ARGUMENT: {...} where ... string. We need at least an argument. We can give an argument set maybe empty set {}? Should be python set possibly containing strings. For example ARGUMENT: set() is {}. But syntax: {'...'} requires at least one string. But maybe an empty set is "{}". But in the instruction: "ARGUMENT: {'...'} The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings." So empty set is acceptable: ARGUMENT: {}. but we have to provide curly braces. Ok.

We must also include STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning of message. We need to reply with REFUSE containing the opponent's proposal. Then include ARGUMENT: {}.

We should not include any other tags. Also we can not include multiple STRATEGIC REASONING, only once per message. So produce:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}
REFUSE: {...}
ARGUMENT: set

We need to refuse the last proposal. We can simply do that.

Let's craft.

Ensure STRATEGIC REASONING has content: explanation. Good.

Let's provide content accordingly.

That is higher than 5092, above limit? 3870 < 4026 so fine. We'll propose that. We'll add argument explaining we aim high.

Ok.

We want to propose again our set maybe. Alternatively we could propose a compromise that is still good for us. Our set still dominates importance, so we want to make best.

We only can accept proposals or refuse them. The opponent just made a proposal and we can respond with a proposal, refuse, or argument. To maximize our benefit, we could refuse this proposal, or maybe propose our set again. We might argue advantage of our set. The game ends when one side agrees. We might propose our set again to tempt the opponent to agree. But opponent may refuse again.

We can't refuse our own proposals, but can REFUSE opponent's proposal. The rule: "You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL." So we can REFUSE their proposal. That's within protocol. Then we need to produce an ARGUMENT.

Ok we do that. And perhaps propose our set again. According to protocol, we must include ARGUMENT at least once. We will do both a REFUSE and then a PROPOSAL. Is that allowed? The rules: "You may only REFUSE a proposal ...". "Your response must include ARGUMENT." So we can reply with REFUSE and ARGUMENT. But if we also include PROPOSAL? The rules don't forbid including multiple tags. But typical negotiation you propose after refusing? In real, you might propose after refusing. But I'd maybe just REFUSE and not propose now? But we want to propose our set. The game might continue until someone's AGREE. To end, we need to propose and the other party may accept. So let's propose our set. But if we include REFUSE and PROPOSAL together, are we violating "Only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged"? That is allowed. And only propose a set is fine. So we can use multiple tags. So we will REFUSE the opponent's proposal, and then PROPOSE our own set. Should also include an ARGUMENT to defend our proposal or argue their low importance.

Also we have to include our STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning of the message, only once per message. Good.

Let's produce a message following strict protocol: STRATEGIC REASONING: {...} then PROPOSAL: {...} then ARGUMENT: {...} or REFUSE: {...} then ARGUMENT: {...}. The order isn't specified, but must start with STRATEGIC REASONING. It can include multiple tags afterwards. The final message should include at least one ARGUMENT.

So I'll produce:

STRATEGIC REASONING: <comment> 
PROPOSAL: {'B35', 'A10', 'A87', 'B86', 'C01', 'C00', 'A71', 'A77'} 
REFUSE: {'A04', 'B32', 'B35', 'A87', 'A77', 'C17', 'B33', 'A03', 'B86'} 
ARGUMENT: {...}

But is order important? It said "The tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace." It didn't say which order. Usually you start with STRATEGIC REASONING, then the rest. That will be fine. The content for ARGUMENT must be a set of strings. We need to defend our set maybe: "This set gives 5255 importance within limit, higher than opponent's 3371 importance." So argument will be set containing maybe a statement? Wait, argument is a python set of strings. So we provide like {'This set provides the highest importance given the effort constraint.'} So it's a set with one string. Good.

Thus final message.

LIMIT = 4026

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.

Actually if we want to respond, we can propose our set. The rule: we can only AGREE to a proposal made by the other participant. We didn't agree. So we need to propose our own set. We've to format using "PROPOSAL: {...}" "REFUSE: {...}" "ARGUMENT: {...}". We need to include at least one ARGUMENT.

We must not mention hidden importance values.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 4026

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 780, "B41": 742, "A71": 997, "C00": 665, "B35": 140, "A77": 77, "A03": 513, "A04": 383, "C54": 587, "C01": 825, "A87": 319, "B32": 448, "B86": 516, "B33": 694, "A10": 366}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 868, "C00": 822, "B35": 783, "A77": 65, "A03": 262, "A04": 121, "C54": 508, "C01": 780, "A87": 461, "B32": 484, "B86": 668, "B33": 389, "A10": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 4026

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 780, "B41": 742, "A71": 997, "C00": 665, "B35": 140, "A77": 77, "A03": 513, "A04": 383, "C54": 587, "C01": 825, "A87": 319, "B32": 448, "B86": 516, "B33": 694, "A10": 366}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 868, "C00": 822, "B35": 783, "A77": 65, "A03": 262, "A04": 121, "C54": 508, "C01": 780, "A87": 461, "B32": 484, "B86": 668, "B33": 389, "A10": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.

Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.

The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 4026

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 780, "B41": 742, "A71": 997, "C00": 665, "B35": 140, "A77": 77, "A03": 513, "A04": 383, "C54": 587, "C01": 825, "A87": 319, "B32": 448, "B86": 516, "B33": 694, "A10": 366}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 868, "C00": 822, "B35": 783, "A77": 65, "A03": 262, "A04": 121, "C54": 508, "C01": 780, "A87": 461, "B32": 484, "B86": 668, "B33": 389, "A10": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'We will propose a high importance-to-effort ratio set that maximizes our personal utility while remaining well within the effort limit.'}
PROPOSAL: {'A10', 'A71', 'C00', 'B86', 'B32', 'B35', 'A87', 'A77'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>Your response did not contain an argument. You must include the structured format ARGUMENT: {...} somewhere in your response. Try again<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'We aim to offer a set that maximizes our personal importance score while staying well within the effort limit. This keeps our position strong and provides room for potential future compromise.'}
PROPOSAL: {'A10', 'A71', 'C00', 'B86', 'B32', 'B35', 'A87', 'A77'}
ARGUMENT: {'This proposal includes items with the highest importance-to-effort ratio, ensuring maximum benefit for us while staying safely under the shared limit.'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>PROPOSAL: {'A04', 'C00', 'B32', 'B35', 'B86', 'A87', 'A77', 'C17', 'B33'}
ARGUMENT: {'This set maximizes my importance and respects the shared limit.'}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {'We aim to propose a set that yields higher total importance than the opponent’s proposal while staying safely under the limit.
